content:
# Biyadh Formation

**Summary**: Geological formation studied in the thesis collection.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-al-haidary|Al-Haidary, 2014]] studied the Biyadh Formation

- [[2017-khan|Khan, 2017]] used the Biyadh sandstone as a primary case study for single-porosity carbonate reservoir modeling.

- [[2018-abdulrahman|Abdulrahman, 2018]] analyzed the Biyadh formation to evaluate the impact of offset on its velocity estimation.

## Depositional Environment



- [[2017-khan|Khan, 2017]] used the Biyadh sandstone as a primary case study for single-porosity carbonate reservoir modeling.

- [[2018-abdulrahman|Abdulrahman, 2018]] analyzed the Biyadh formation to evaluate the impact of offset on its velocity estimation.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- studied the Biyadh Formation ([[2014-al-haidary|Al-Haidary, 2014]])

- The Biyadh sandstone was modeled with an initial porosity of 0.12 and a Young's Modulus of 44.7 GPa. ([[2017-khan|Khan, 2017, p. 35]])

- The Biyadh has a mean velocity of 4045 m/s and a thickness of 197 meters at x=18000m. ([[2018-abdulrahman|Abdulrahman, 2018, p. 22]])
